rag_rules: Attach each generated annex block to the AADL owner whose scope makes all referenced identifiers visible.
Keep type-scope contract content on component types, and keep implementation-only bindings or path-based references on component implementations.
generic_rules: Preserve the existing architecture verbatim: do not change subcomponents, connections, features, properties, or declaration names while inserting an annex.
Make the smallest possible structural edit: add the annex to the correct owner without moving unrelated declarations.
If generated content depends on implementation-local paths or bindings, fuse it into the implementation owner rather than the type owner.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Insert a complete `annex agree {** ... **};` block inside the chosen owner; when implementation content depends on local declarations, place the block after those relevant declarations in that owner.
anti_patterns: Do not place subcomponent-port references or other implementation-only paths inside a type-scope annex.
Do not duplicate the same generated block across both type and implementation scopes.
irrelevant_topics: Expression-level contract examples such as selector or clamping patterns
